message:
refactor(cli): hoist --robot-format to global flag, extend secret redaction to snippets/titles/metadata, add snippet export migration, fix SFTP sync paths, and centralize TUI layout cache invalidation

**Global --robot-format consolidation (src/lib.rs)**

The `--json` / `--robot` output flag was duplicated across 20+ subcommands
(search, stats, diag, status, health, doctor, view, export, expand, timeline,
capabilities, introspect, api-version, state, sessions, context, sources list,
sources doctor, sources sync, sources discover, sources setup, models status,
models verify, models check-update, mappings list, analytics, import chatgpt).
Every subcommand had its own `#[arg(long, visible_alias = "robot")] json: bool`
declaration, and every handler independently constructed the structured format
via an identical `if json { Some(RobotFormat::Json) } else { robot_format_from_env() }`
block — duplicated ~20 times across thousands of lines.

This change lifts `--robot-format` to a global CLI argument on the `Cli` struct
so it applies uniformly to all subcommands. Per-subcommand `--json` flags are
removed (or bound to `_` where clap still needs the field for backwards compat).
Each handler now reads `cli.robot_format.or_else(robot_format_from_env)` once,
eliminating the scattered duplication.  The `is_robot_mode()` function similarly
reads from the global `cli.robot_format` instead of pattern-matching on each
subcommand's `json` field.

This matters because adding a new output format previously required editing
every subcommand definition and handler — a classic shotgun-surgery antipattern.
Now new formats only need to be added to the `RobotFormat` enum.

**Secret redaction coverage for titles, metadata, and snippets (src/indexer/mod.rs)**

The `persist` module's conversation serialization previously only redacted
message content when `should_redact` was true, but passed conversation titles,
metadata JSON, and code snippet text through verbatim. Secrets (API keys,
tokens, passwords) can appear in any of these fields — a session title might
contain a leaked key from a user prompt, metadata might echo back environment
variables, and snippet text captures source code that may contain hardcoded
credentials. All three fields are now run through `redact_secrets::redact_text`
when redaction is enabled, closing these coverage gaps.

**Snippet scanning in secret scanner (src/pages/secret_scan.rs)**

The secret scanner (`scan_database`) queried conversations, messages, and
metadata but did not scan the `snippets` table at all. This meant code snippets
containing secrets (e.g. hardcoded AWS keys in source files) would pass the
scan undetected. A new `MessageSnippet` variant is added to `SecretLocation`,
and a full scan pass over the snippets table is appended to the scanner. The
scan respects the same truncation, cancellation, and progress-bar contracts as
the existing passes.

**Export snippet migration (src/pages/export.rs)**

The export engine creates a standalone SQLite database for portable
conversation archives. It previously created `conversations`, `messages`, and
`export_meta` tables but omitted the `snippets` table entirely, silently
dropping all code-snippet data during export. A `snippets` table with proper
foreign keys to `messages` is now created in the export schema, and snippet
rows are migrated alongside their parent messages during the copy loop
(tagged bd-4x92 for traceability).

**SFTP sync path handling fix (src/sources/sync.rs)**

The SFTP fallback sync path had a directory-structure bug: when downloading a
remote directory, it would write files directly into the container directory
rather than creating a subdirectory named after the remote path's leaf. For
example, syncing `/home/user/.claude/sessions` would dump session files
directly into the source's local directory instead of into a `sessions/`
subdirectory, conflicting with other synced paths that share the same
container. The fix mirrors rsync/scp behavior by appending the leaf directory
name. The single-file download path is also corrected to use the target path
directly rather than re-extracting the filename, and parent directory creation
is moved to the correct scope.

**TUI layout cache invalidation on resize (src/ui/app.rs)**

Hit-test rectangle caches (`last_pill_rects`, `last_pane_rects`,
`last_saved_view_row_areas`, `last_suggestion_rects`, `last_pane_first_index`)
were being cleared in scattered locations: once at the start of every
`render_search_surface` call, once when entering the Analytics surface, and
once when entering the Sources surface. This caused two problems: (1) on
terminal resize, stale rectangles from the previous size persisted until the
next surface transition, causing mouse clicks to target wrong regions; (2) the
per-frame clear in `render_search_surface` was wasteful since the caches are
immediately rebuilt during the same frame.

The fix centralizes all cache clearing into a single resize-detection block
at the top of the view function. A `last_terminal_size` cell tracks the
previous frame dimensions; when a resize is detected, all layout-dependent
caches are invalidated once. The scattered per-surface and per-frame clears
are removed.

Additionally, the `FilterTimeSet` handler now correctly updates `time_preset`
to `TimePreset::All` when both bounds are cleared or `TimePreset::Custom` when
explicit bounds are set, preventing the time filter pill from showing a stale
preset label after programmatic filter changes. Minor formatting cleanup on
`FilterTimeSet` message construction brings it in line with the project's
multi-line struct literal style.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -125,6 +125,20 @@ impl ExportEngine {
                 )
                 .context("Failed to create messages table")?;
 
+                tx.execute(
+                    "CREATE TABLE snippets (
+                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
+                message_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
+                file_path TEXT,
+                start_line INTEGER,
+                end_line INTEGER,
+                language TEXT,
+                snippet_text TEXT,
+                FOREIGN KEY (message_id) REFERENCES messages(id)
+            )",
+                )
+                .context("Failed to create snippets table")?;
+
                 tx.execute(
                     "CREATE TABLE export_meta (
                 key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
@@ -344,6 +358,29 @@ impl ExportEngine {
                             params![*source_message_id, content.as_str()],
                         )?;
 
+                        // 5. Migrate Snippets for this message (bd-4x92)
+                        let snip_rows: Vec<(Option<String>, Option<i64>, Option<i64>, Option<String>, String)> = src.query_map_collect(
+                            "SELECT file_path, start_line, end_line, language, snippet_text FROM snippets WHERE message_id = ?1",
+                            params![*source_message_id],
+                            |row: &FrankenRow| {
+                                Ok((
+                                    row.get_typed::<Option<String>>(0)?,
+                                    row.get_typed::<Option<i64>>(1)?,
+                                    row.get_typed::<Option<i64>>(2)?,
+                                    row.get_typed::<Option<String>>(3)?,
+                                    row.get_typed::<String>(4)?,
+                                ))
+                            },
+                        )?;
+
+                        for (fpath, start, end, lang, stext) in snip_rows {
+                            tx.execute_compat(
+                                "INSERT INTO snippets (message_id, file_path, start_line, end_line, language, snippet_text)
+                                 VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6)",
+                                params![*source_message_id, fpath, start, end, lang, stext.as_str()],
+                            )?;
+                        }
+
                         msg_processed += 1;
                     }
